frontmatter.description: Bullet list of unrelated observations that share no trigger condition.

asset.body:
- The build is slow on Tuesdays.
- The kitchen coffee machine needs descaling.
- @kira prefers PRs split into commits of <300 lines.
- The hot-path benchmark sometimes errors out under high load.
- Reminder to update the team OKR doc before the next planning.
- The legacy queue worker still uses Redis 6.

feedback.reason: Useful as a junk drawer of notes
feedback.note: Not really a lesson, more a sticky note.

feedback.reason: When distilled, the resulting lesson description and when_to_use cannot be made coherent because the observations have no shared trigger

humanGrade.rationale: The asset is a bag of unrelated observations. A well-instructed LLM following the LESSON prompt cannot produce a single coherent `when_to_use` — the prompt explicitly forbids circular `When working with <asset>` phrasings. The most likely failure mode is the LLM emits an invalid frontmatter (e.g. missing description or when_to_use), which trips lesson-lint and produces `validation_failed`. Score range reflects that a strict judge would also catch this before validation, but the modal failure here is structural.
